title: selenothamnus helmsii
intro:
Selenothamnus helmsii, also known as the 'stink bush', is a flowering plant species endemic to Western Australia, characterized by its strong and unpleasant odor.

The medicinal properties of Selenothamnus helmsii involve the use of its leaves and stems in traditional Aboriginal medicine, which have been reported to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.

Some of the health benefits associated with Selenothamnus helmsii include its potential to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation.

Traditional herbal preparations of Selenothamnus helmsii involve boiling the leaves and stems in water to create a tea or infusion, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
